title: Allstate to Discuss Second Quarter 2023 Earnings With Investors
text: NORTHBROOK, Ill.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Allstate Corporation (NYSE: ALL) will conduct a conference call and webcast at 11:00 a.m. Eastern on Wednesday, Aug. 2, 2023, to discuss second quarter 2023 earnings. The company plans to file a current report on Form 8-K with the Securities and Exchange Commission announcing quarterly results at or after 4:15 p.m. Eastern on Tuesday, Aug. 1. title: AXS or ALL: Which Is the Better Value Stock Right Now?

title: Allstate (ALL) Continues Auto Rate Hikes to Combat Inflation
text: Allstate (ALL) reports estimated catastrophe losses of $699 million after taxes in May.

title: Allstate Announces May 2023 Catastrophe Losses and Implemented Auto Rates
text: NORTHBROOK, Ill.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Allstate Corporation (NYSE: ALL) today announced estimated catastrophe losses for the month of May of $885 million or $699 million, after-tax. May month catastrophe losses include 12 events estimated at $893 million, with approximately 70% of the losses related to two wind and hail events, partially offset by favorable reserve reestimates for prior events. Total catastrophe losses for April and May were $1.68 billion, pre-tax. During the month of May, the.

title: The 7 Best Stocks to Buy in June for the Looming Recession
text: Although recent data points suggest that investors should anticipate a robust bull market ahead, a potentially upcoming plot twist might favor the best stocks for looming recession. At first, such a notion seems ridiculous primarily because of the most recent May jobs report.

title: Allstate: New 7.375% Preferred Shares
text: Allstate issued new 7.375% preferred shares, which started trading under the symbol, ALL.PJ. We examine the yield in relation to other similar rated instruments and also to other Allstate preferred shares. We give our buy under price for the ALL.PJ shares.

title: AXS vs. ALL: Which Stock Is the Better Value Option?
text: Investors interested in stocks from the Insurance - Property and Casualty sector have probably already heard of Axis Capital (AXS) and Allstate (ALL). But which of these two stocks is more attractive to value investors?

title: One Single New Preferred Stock IPO This Month
text: Tightening financial conditions have led to a significant decrease in preferred stock and ETD offering activity, with only one new preferred stock offered in May. Allstate issued non-cumulative perpetual preferred shares with a fixed 7.375% dividend rate, while Gladstone Investment Corporation offered $65 million worth of 8% senior notes due 2028. CDx3 preferred stocks ranked 10 out of 10 are selling at an average discount to par of about 7.4% and offer an average current yield of 6.37%.
